session_context: John Mitchell impersonating Emily Johnson (Camila-incorporate org) — pre-existing session from earlier test run; David Kim impersonation also tested via silent-path redirect

top_contributors.note: Large hero/features block causes massive layout shift on load

top_contributors.class: flex flex-col items-center justify-between gap-1 bg-[--footer-bottom-bg-color]...

BL-UI-001_CLS.note: Homepage features-block contributes 0.449 CLS — this is a page-level issue not specific to impersonation, but is measured here as the post-impersonation landing page

impersonation_banner.note: Banner row uses gap:4px between inline items — on-grid

BL-UI-003_state_shift.note: Banner top row rect stable across pages: top:7 h:26 w:252 on /, /catalog, /account/orders

impersonation_banner_mobile.description: At mobile viewport (375-500px), the entire impersonation top bar (John Mitchell logged in as [Target]) is hidden — 0×0px visible area. No impersonation indicator is visible to the operator on mobile.
impersonation_banner_mobile.impact: High — operator cannot see they are in an impersonated session on mobile
impersonation_banner_mobile.note: Classified under BL-UI-004 (content stays in container / content not silently hidden)

impersonation_row_1280.note: Perfect alignment — all three inline elements share centerY=19.5

at_mobile_375px.account_menu_button: NOT VISIBLE — hidden at mobile breakpoint
at_mobile_375px.logout_button: NOT VISIBLE — hidden at mobile breakpoint
at_mobile_375px.back_to_operator_button: NOT VISIBLE — hidden at mobile breakpoint
at_mobile_375px.impact: Operator cannot exit impersonation session on mobile — no stop-impersonation affordance available

banner_stickiness.note: Banner scrolls away on long pages. Operator loses impersonation indicator after scrolling past the top bar.
